title: Combustor_Diffuser_Modeling_Part_I_Inlet_Profiles__2-D_Calculations
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 3]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   ∂(ρk)/∂t + ∂(ρku_i)/∂x_i = ∂/∂x_j [(μ + μ_t/σ_k) ∂k/∂x_j] + G_k + G_b - ρε - Y_m
*   ∂(ρε)/∂t + ∂(ρεu_i)/∂x_i = ∂/∂x_j [(μ + μ_t/σ_ε) ∂ε/∂x_j] + C_1ε ε/k (G_k + C_3ε G_b) - C_2ε ρ ε^2/k
*   G_k = -ρu_i u'_j ∂u_i/∂x_j
*   G_b = βg_i μ_t/Pr_t ∂T/∂x_i
*   Y_m = 2ρεM_t^2
*   M_t = √k/α^2
*   μ_t = ρC_μ k^2/ε
*   U^+ = 1/κ ln(Ey^+)
*   U^+ = U_p C_μ^(1/4) k_p^(1/2)/(τ_w/ρ)^(1/2)
*   y^+ = ρC_μ^(1/4) k_p^(1/2) y_p/μ
*   U^+ = y^+

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**3. Tables:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**4. Diagrams:**

NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   σ = 2.1% and 8.1%
*   variations of Cp and 2 between the nine configurations of the orders of 9.1% and 27.4%
*   root-mean square errors of ~5% and 15%
*   κ (von Karman constant) = 0.42
*   E (empirical constant) = 9.81
*   C_1ε = 1.44
*   C_2ε = 1.92
*   C_μ = 0.09
*   σ_k = 1.00
*   σ_ε = 1.30
*   y^+ > 11.225 logarithmic law for mean velocity
*   y^+ < 11.225 laminar stress-strain relationship

title: Combustor_Diffuser_Modeling_Part_I_Inlet_Profiles__2-D_Calculations
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 4]
1. Mathematical equations:

G_k = tau_w * dU/dy = tau_w^2 / (rho * C_mu^1/4 * k_p^1/2 * y_p)

epsilon_p = C_mu^3/4 * k_p^3/2 / (kappa * y_p)

(U * C_mu^1/4 * k^1/2) / (tau_w / rho) = 1/kappa * ln(E * (rho * C_mu^1/4 * k^1/2 * y) / mu)

U_tilde = U - (1 / (2 * dx) * dp/dx * (y_v / (rho * kappa * sqrt(k))) * ln(y/y_v) + (y - y_v) / (rho * kappa * sqrt(k)) + (y^2/mu)

y_v = (mu * y_v_star) / (rho * C_mu^1/4 * k_p^1/2)

y_v_star = 11.225

tau_t = { 0, y < y_v
        tau_w, y > y_v }

k = { (y/y_v)^2 * k_p, y < y_v
       k_p, y > y_v }

epsilon = { (2 *nu * k) / y^2, y < y_v
            (k^3/2) / (C_1 * y), y > y_v}

C_1 = kappa * C_mu^-3/4

G_bar_k = integral(tau * dU/dy dy)

G_bar_k = (1/(kappa * nu) )* (tau_w^2 / (rho * C_mu^1/4 * k_p^1/2)) * ln(y_n /  y_v)

epsilon_bar = 1/y_n * integral(epsilon dy) = 1 / y_n * [ (2 *nu * k) /y + (k^1/2) / C_1 * ln(y/y_v) ] * k_p

∂/∂t (ρk) + ∂/∂xi (ρkiui) = ∂/∂xj [Γk ∂k/∂xj] + Gk - Yk
∂/∂t (ρω) + ∂/∂xi (ρωui) = ∂/∂xj [Γω ∂ω/∂xj] + Gw - Yω + Dω

2. Graphs/charts: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

3. Tables: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

4. Diagrams: NO_VISUAL_CONTENT

5. Key numbers, constants, parameters:

k: turbulence kinetic energy
epsilon: dissipation rate
G_k: production of k
tau_t: shear stress
y_v: physical viscous sub-layer thickness
y_v_star = 11.225
y_n: height of the cell
y_p: ???
kappa: Constant (von Karman constant)
C_mu: Constant
rho: density
mu: molecular viscosity
nu: kinematic viscosity
---

title: Combustor_Diffuser_Modeling_Part_I_Inlet_Profiles__2-D_Calculations
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 5]
**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Gk = -ρui (∂u/∂xi)
*   Gw = -α*(ω/k)Gk
*   α = α∞*(α0 + Ret/Rω)/(α∞ + Ret/Rω)
*   α0 = (α*∞ + Ret/Rk)/(1 + Ret/Rk)
*   Re = (ρk)/(μω)
*   Γk = μ + (μt/σk)
*   Γω = μ + (μt/σω)
*   μt = (ρk/ω) * (1/max[α*(α1/ω), ΩF2])
*   Ω = √(2ΩijΩij)
*   σk = 1/[F1/σk,1 + (1 - F1)/σk,2]
*   σω = 1/[F1/σω,1 + (1 - F1)/σω,2]
*   F1 = tanh(Φ1^4)
*   Φ1 = min[max(√k/(0.09ωy), 500μ/(py²ω)), (4σk)/(σω,2Dω^*)]
*   Dω^* = max[(2ρ)/(σω,2) (∂k/∂xj)(∂ω/∂xj), 10^(-20)]
*   F2 = tanh(Φ2^2)
*   Φ2 = max[(2√kρ)/(0.09ωy), (500μ)/(py²ω)]
*   Yk = ρβ^*fβ^*kω
*   Yω = ρβ^*fβ^*ω²
*   fβ^* = { 1 + 680xk^2  if xk<=0 ,  1 + 400xk^2 if xk>0}
*   xk = (1/ω^3)(∂k/∂xj)(∂ω/∂xj)
*   β^* = β^*[1 +ζ*F(Mt)]
*   βi^* = β∞^* ( (4/15 + (Re/Rβ)^4)/(1 + (Re/Rβ)^4) )
*   fβ^* = (1 + 70xω)/(1 + 80xω)
*   xω = (|ΩijΩjkSki|)/((β*∞ω)^3)

**2. Key Numbers, Constants, Parameters:**

*   Rk = 6
*   α*0 = βi/3,
*   βi=0.072
*   ζ\* = 1.5
*   Rβ = 8
*   β∗∞ = 0.09

**3. Other**

*   Ωij is the mean rate-of-rotation tensor
*   σk and σω are turbulent Prandtl numbers for k and ω.
*   Yk and Yω represent the dissipation of k and ω due to turbulence.

title: Combustor_Diffuser_Modeling_Part_I_Inlet_Profiles__2-D_Calculations
text:
[VISUAL/EQUATION CONTENT - Page 6]
Here's the technical content extracted from the provided text:

**1. Mathematical Equations:**

*   Ωij = 1/2(∂ui/∂xj + ∂uj/∂xi)
*   β = βi[1 − (βi/β*)ζ*F(Mi)] where F(Mi) is the compressibility correction.
*   Dω = 2(1 − F1)ρσω,2 (1/ω) (∂k/∂xj) (∂ω/∂xj )
*   l = 0.07Dh.
*   k = (3/2)(uavg I)²
*   ε = Cμ3/4 (k^(3/2))/l

**2. Graphs/Charts:**

*   The text mentions Figures 2, 3a, 3b, 3c, and 3d which presumably contain plots of:
    *   Iso-axial velocity contours and circumferential variation in the axial velocity (Fig 2).
    *   Contours of total pressure (Fig. 3a)
    *   Contours of turbulence kinetic energy (Fig. 3b).
    *   Circumferential variation of *k* (Fig. 3c)
    *   Circumferential variation of *ε* (Fig. 3d)
    * No axes, units or specific curves are specified in the text.

**3. Tables:**
NO_TABLES_PRESENT

**4. Diagrams:**

*   Fig. 1b: Bow-tie profiler geometries (3-dimensional in nature)
*   Fig. 1e: 2-D axi-symmetric simulation (40k cells generated with y+ ~ 40)
*   Fig. 1d: All hexahedral mesh comprised of ~512k cells
*   Fig. 1c: Inlet section (20×96 cells, 18º sector, channel height of 0.76")

**5. Key Numbers, Constants, and Parameters:**

*   α*∞ = 1, α∞ = 0.52, αω∞ = 1/9, β*∞ = 0.09, βi = 0.072, Rβ = 8, Rk = 6, Rω = 2.95, ζ* = 1.5, Mt0 = 0.25, σκ = 2, σω = 2, σκ,1 = 1.176, σω,1 = 2.0, σκ,2 = 1.0, σω,2 = 1.168, α1 = 0.31, βi,1 = 0.075, βi,2 = 0.0828
*   y+ ~ 40 (for k-e model)
*   y+ ~ 1 (for SST k-ω model)
*   Axial length of gridded section: 9.2"
*   Channel height at inlet: 0.76"
*   Minimum cell size near profiler walls: 0.0155" (axial), 0.033" (radial)
*   Dimensions of upstream dump: 8h * 5h (where h = 0.764")
